embeds.description:
🟢 **All Systems Operational**

Shared DM queue between both bots. Every outgoing DM from any cog is intercepted and inserted into a shared SQLite database. A coordinator loop assigns each DM to whichever bot has capacity, enabling automatic load balancing. The worker loop on each bot picks up its assigned DMs and delivers them.

📋 `dm_shared_queue.db` • Bots online: **2/2** • Refreshes every 30s

before.content:
Here's the pull-on-Windows playbook + what landed in master:

Pull command (works on Windows too)
In your bot folder on Windows (probably C:\...\Wave-Management-Bot or similar):

cd path\to\Wave-Management-Bot
git pull origin master
That's it — git pull is identical on Windows and Mac.

What you'll receive in the pull
3 new commits since 5c4746c:

Commit    What it does    File(s)
b52f8c7    Adds project-level CLAUDE.md (file map + system architecture)    CLAUDE.md (new)
0f98bde    Removes hardcoded Anthropic API key    main.py
ea51010    Replaces Anthropic API call with template pool    main.py, tasks/random_challenges.py
Post-pull actions on Windows
Restart the bot — tasks/random_challenges.py and main.py were both changed, so the bot needs to reload.
Optional cleanup — anthropic Python package is no longer imported. You can uninstall it to slim down deps:
pip uninstall anthropic
(Skip this if you have other code on the bot that uses it.)
No .env change needed — ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is no longer used by the bot, so you don't need to set it. If you had it in .env, you can leave it (harmless) or delete it.
What's on GitHub master right now
ea51010 Remove Anthropic API dependency from random_challenges  ← HEAD
0f98bde Remove hardcoded Anthropic API key from main.py
b52f8c7 Add project-level CLAUDE.md with file map and system architecture
5c4746c Add weights folder with drop spot marker and glider lines models
Run this on Windows after pulling to confirm you got all 3:

git log --oneline -4
Still to do (independent of pull)
🔴 Revoke the leaked API key at https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys — the key string sk-ant-api03-VI8wSFSF... is still in git history (commits before 0f98bde). Anyone with repo access can fish it out. Revoking it makes the leaked string useless.

Want me to walk through the revoke flow or also handle the git-history purge?

after.content:
Here's the pull-on-Windows playbook + what landed in master:

Pull command (works on Windows too)
In your bot folder on Windows (probably C:\...\Wave-Management-Bot or similar):

cd path\to\Wave-Management-Bot
git pull origin master
That's it — git pull is identical on Windows and Mac.

What you'll receive in the pull
3 new commits since 5c4746c:

Commit    What it does    File(s)
b52f8c7    Adds project-level CLAUDE.md (file map + system architecture)    CLAUDE.md (new)
0f98bde    Removes hardcoded Anthropic API key    main.py
ea51010    Replaces Anthropic API call with template pool    main.py, tasks/random_challenges.py
Post-pull actions on Windows
Restart the bot — tasks/random_challenges.py and main.py were both changed, so the bot needs to reload.
Optional cleanup — anthropic Python package is no longer imported. You can uninstall it to slim down deps:
pip uninstall anthropic
(Skip this if you have other code on the bot that uses it.)
No .env change needed — ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is no longer used by the bot, so you don't need to set it. If you had it in .env, you can leave it (harmless) or delete it.
What's on GitHub master right now
ea51010 Remove Anthropic API dependency from random_challenges  ← HEAD
0f98bde Remove hardcoded Anthropic API key from main.py
b52f8c7 Add project-level CLAUDE.md with file map and system architecture
5c4746c Add weights folder with drop spot marker and glider lines models
Run this on Windows after pulling to confirm you got all 3:

git log --oneline -4
Still to do (independent of pull)
🔴 Revoke the leaked API key at https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys — the key string sk-ant-api03-VI8wSFSF... is still in git history (commits before 0f98bde). Anyone with repo access can fish it out. Revoking it makes the leaked string useless.
